front: Poem: 'We must love one another or die'; set 'in one of the dives / On Fifty-second Street' as WWII begins
Poem: 'the clever hopes expire / of a low dishonest decade'; 'the lights must never go out / the music must always play'
Poem: 'Stop all the clocks, cut off the telephone'; the deceased was 'my North, my South, my East and West'
Poem: 'She looked over his shoulder' repeats in an ekphrastic poem where 'an artificial wilderness / And a sky like lead' replaces 'marble well-governed cities'
Poem: 'About suffering they were never wrong, / The Old Masters'; a ploughman 'may / Have heard the splash' in Brueghel's Icarus
Poem: declares 'poetry makes nothing happen' in an elegy for a man who died on a 'dark cold day'
back: "In Memory of W. B. Yeats" (W. H. Auden)
work: In Memory of W. B. Yeats

front: Poem: 'I'll love you / Till Africa and China meet'; advises 'Let not Time deceive you, / You cannot conquer time'
back: "As I Walked Out One Evening" (W. H. Auden)
work: As I Walked Out One Evening

front: Poem: 'If equal affection cannot be / Let the more loving one be me'; opens 'Looking up at the stars, I know quite well'
Author: co-wrote plays set in fictional Ostnia (The Ascent of F6, The Dog Beneath the Skin) with Christopher Isherwood
Poem: 'Night Mail' describes a train 'bringing the cheque and the postal order'; ends 'for who can bear to feel themselves forgotten?'
Poem: mocked Apollo 11 as a 'phallic triumph'
